name: Autoplaying media
summary: Video or audio starts playing automatically on page load without user consent, interrupting the task, burning data, and often displaying ads that the user cannot dismiss without interaction.

evidence.claim: Chrome 66+ (April 2018) blocks autoplay with sound by default; Safari 11+ enforces the same. Violating sites typically mute the track but still autoplay video, which is itself a data-cost and accessibility harm.
FTC 2022 'Bringing Dark Patterns to Light' report identifies autoplaying content that forces interaction as a Section 5 deceptive practice when paired with ads or upsell.

W3C WCAG 2.1 Success Criterion 1.4.2 (Audio Control): if audio plays automatically for more than 3 seconds, a mechanism to pause or stop is required. Autoplay without such mechanism fails WCAG Level A.

body.description: Video or audio plays automatically when a page loads — ad pre-rolls, muted-but-animated 'feature showcase' videos, background music, or video pop-outs that follow the viewport as the user scrolls. Beyond the interruption, autoplay burns mobile data (30-100MB per auto-played video), triggers CPU fan noise, and violates WCAG 1.4.2 when paired with audio (E3).

detectionHeuristics.description: HTML5 autoplay attribute on media element.
JavaScript calling .play() without user interaction — often via IntersectionObserver trigger that fires immediately.
Viewport-following sticky videos that persist across page interactions.

body.llmVerifyPrompt: Inspect the page for autoplaying media. Check: (1) does media start without explicit user click, (2) does audio play without a visible pause control within 3 seconds (WCAG 1.4.2 violation), (3) is the media ad content, (4) is the media a sticky/floating element that persists on scroll. Flag verdict: no-autoplay, muted-autoplay, or intrusive-autoplay.
body.remediation: Overlay Lens warning with one-click action to (a) pause all media on the page via browser API, (b) enable browser-level autoplay blocking (Chrome/Firefox settings deep-link), (c) cite WCAG 1.4.2 if the autoplay has audio.
